MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Plaintiff'S Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Sep-18-2017 Continued To Nov-17-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

Plaintiff'S Dewinter Group'S Renewed Motion For A Right To Attach Order And Writ Of Attachment
Off Calendar Sep-21-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, David J. Cook of the law offices of Cook Collection Attorneys PLC (415-989-4730) appears as counsel for plaintiff/moving party

Attorney, Dhaivat H. Shah of the law offices of Grellas Shah LLP (408-255-6310) appears as counsel for defendant/opposing party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Dewinter Group's Renewed Motion For A Right To Attach Order And Writ Of Attachment against defendant Virool, Inc is GRANTED to the amount of 131,553.31. The claimed amount of $148,322.69 is reduced by $7,769.38 to reflect Virool's payment to DeWinter and by $9,000 to reflect the estimated allowable attorneys fees . Plaintiff has the burden of establishing it is entitled to the order. Bank of America v. Salinas Nissan, Inc. (1989) 207 Cal.App.3d 260, 273. DeWineter has to show that requirements for issuing the order are satisfied and plaintiff's claim is probably valid. Cal. Code Civ. Proc. 484.050(b). Attachment is appropriate because the claims are based on a contract for services provided and the amount is readily ascertainable from the invoices submitted into evidence. CCP 483.010(a). The property sought to be attached is adequately described and the assets sought to be attached are not exempt. The attachment is not sought for purposes other than recovery on the claim upon which the attachment is based. Cal. Civ. Proc. Code 484.090. Plaintiff has shown probable validity of its claims for reasonable value for service provided, breach of contract and account stated. The contractual relationship between plaintiff and defendant is undisputed. (Debelov's Declaration). DeWinter has submitted evidence that the services contracted for were performed by DeWinter. (Costello's Declaration) . DeWinter has submitted evidence for invoices billed. (Sullivan Declaration). Virool's main dispute relates to the overtime charges claimed. The evidentiary support to Virool's opposition is Mr. Debelov's declaration showing Mr Debelov's doubts about the correctness of the overtime charge and this evidence is not sufficient to overcome the probable validly of Dewinter's claim supported by DeWinter's evidence. The amount of the attachment shall be reduced by the amount of $7,769.38 that Virool shows was payed to plaintiff. Although DeWinter's claims that the payment made by Virool should not reduce the amount attached because Virool has not shown it was made on account of the invoices subject to the present action, DeWinter has not alleged any other basis on which the payment was made. The court has discretion to include costs and allowable attorney's fees in the amount secured by the attachment. Civ. Proc. Code 482.110. Plaintiff 's estimated 60 hours of work on the suit filed for enforcement of the contract will be secured in the attachment at a rate of $350 per hour, totaling to $21000. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: James A. Robertson II; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Viscio, CSR No: 12353, email: mviscio@sftc.org. (D302)
